claim_shape: (Currently Amended) An intent-based satellite management system, comprising:one or more satellites in a constellation; a ground-based control system to manage the one or more satellites; a communication module configured to transmit intent-based instruction from the ground-based control system to the one or more satellites, wherein the intent-based instruction comprises one or more predetermined outcomes; and an intent-based module, located on each satellite of the one or more satellites, configured to receive the intent-based instruction and instruct a processor to generate and execute command sequences in order to fulfill the intent-based instruction, wherein the intent- based instruction is based on coarse-grained prediction and fine-grained in-flight measurements. Currently amended 2
remedy: Examiner's ground: Claims 1, 2, 5-6, 12, 15 and 17 are rejected under 35 U.S.C. 102(a)(1) as being anticipated by Schmit et al (US 2021/0,303,290; hereinafter Schmit). Regarding claim 1, Schmit disclose an intent-based satellite management system, comprising: one or more satellites (10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 22) in a constellation; a ground-based control system (30, 32, 50, 52, 54, 56, 60, 62, 64, 66, 68) to manage th
source: USPTO application 18889227, CTNF office action; claims 1, 2, 5, 6, 12, 15, 17 rejected under 35 U.S.C. 102 (a)(1) as being anticipated by Schmit et al (US 2021/0,303,290; hereinafter Schmit)
